title: Move --userns-uid-map/--userns-gid-map  description into buildah man page
body:
Currently we are describing a root global option --userns-uid-map and --userns-gid-map
options in the buildah-bud man page, These global flags can be used in lots of buildah
commands, and should have complete description in the buildah man page.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body: @edsantiago I felt I was spending more time, explaining what I was thinking then actually fixing the code, so I ran in an fixed the code.
LGTM. I will hold #2642 until this merges, then will rebase.
Failure in fedora-31 is a new one to me. Probably a flake, but not one I've seen before.
Can someone re-run this test? I have no privs on this repo.

This is odd: test [fails](https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/task/5836178148556800/logs/integration_test.log) in exactly the same way as before:
```
[+1616s] not ok 430 run --mount
         # $ buildah run --mount type=bind,src=/var/tmp/tmpcc3dead8e6fdb41b732f4835/was:empty,dst=/var/multi-level/subdirectory alpine-working-container touch /var/multi-level/subdirectory/testfile
         # open state file /proc/133569/stat: No such process
         # kill container: No such process
         # error running container: error reading container state (got output: ""): exit status 1
         # error while running runtime: exit status 1
         # [ rc=1 (** EXPECTED 0 **) ]
         # #/vvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvvv
         # #| FAIL: exit code is 1; expected 0
         # #\^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
```
The string `state file` does not appear in the source tree other than in comments; `kill container` does not appear at all. I'm lost.
ah, those errors are coming from `crun`
Merging manually since this is a docs only change.
